mnemonics.title: Combinational vs Sequential
mnemonics.content: Combinational: COPs (Current Output Present) - output is ONLY present input. Sequential: SIPS (State Input Present State) - output depends on State + Input + Previous State.

mnemonics.title: Flip-Flop Types
mnemonics.content: SR: Set-Reset (both 1 = forbidden). JK: Jump Kill (toggle on 11). D: Data/Delay (output follows input). T: Toggle (invert on 1). Remember: 'Some Racers Jump Kingly, Data Drives Timely'.

mnemonics.title: Mealy vs Moore
mnemonics.content: Mealy: Mealy = Meal + E (input triggers output change). Output depends on Input + State. Moore: More = More states (output depends only on State). Remember: 'Mealy Meals at Input, Moore Meals at State'.

mnemonics.title: Latch vs Flip-Flop
mnemonics.content: LATCH = Level-triggered (like a faucet - flows while open). FLIP-FLOP = Edge-triggered (like a light switch - changes on flip). Remember: 'LLL: Latch is Level, Latch is Latch'.

mnemonics.title: Synchronous Sequential
mnemonics.content: Synchronous: All flip-flops share ONE clock (like marching band). Asynchronous: No common clock (like individual runners). Remember: 'Sync = Same Clock'.

acronyms.usage: Model for sequential circuits with finite number of states
Basic flip-flop with Set and Reset inputs
Universal flip-flop that overcomes SR forbidden state
Used for data storage and transfer; simplest memory element
Used in counters and frequency dividers
Hardware description level between behavioral and structural
Early digital logic family (less common usage)
Non-volatile memory used as combinational logic
Sequential circuit used in counters and generators

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box with inputs on left, outputs on right, NO feedback loop lines. Think: Straight pipe - water flows through, no loops back.
Draw inputs → Combinational Logic → Outputs, but ALSO draw feedback path from outputs back to inputs. Add a 'Memory' box (flip-flops) in the feedback loop.
Write truth table → Write Sum-of-Products (SOP) → Simplify using Karnaugh Map (K-Map) → Implement with AND-OR gates. Each '1' in output becomes an AND term.

Draw circles for states (write state name inside). Draw arrows for transitions (label arrow with Input/Condition that causes transition). For Mealy: label arrow as 'Input/Output'. For Moore: label arrow with 'Input' and write output inside circle.

Draw horizontal timeline. Show clock pulses as square waves. Show data lines as flat or changing at clock edges. For flip-flop: show when inputs are sampled (rising/falling edge) and when output changes.
Draw 2x2, 4x4, or 8x8 grid depending on variables. Group adjacent 1s in powers of 2 (1,2,4,8). Overlap allowed. Wrap around edges allowed. Each group eliminates one variable.
Draw rectangle with Q and Q̅ outputs. Show clock input (triangle for edge-trigger). Show preset (PR) and clear (CLR) as inverted inputs. Show data inputs (D, J, K, T, S, R) on left side.

analogies.analogy: A calculator: Press 2+2, you immediately get 4. The answer depends ONLY on current input, no memory of previous calculations.
A bank account balance: Your current balance depends on deposits/withdrawals (current input) PLUS the previous balance (memory). Same input can give different outputs based on past.
A light with two switches (A and B). Switch A turns light ON, switch B turns it OFF. Both ON = ambiguous (don't do this). Both OFF = light stays as it was (remembers last state).
A security guard who writes down who enters at EXACTLY noon each day. Input is who arrives, clock is noon. Output is whatever was written down, stays same until next noon.
A cascade of dominoes: First domino falls (clock), hits second (triggering it), which hits third. Each domino triggers the next WITHOUT waiting for a common signal.
All dancers start moving on the SAME beat of music. Every flip-flop changes state simultaneously at each clock pulse, not in a ripple sequence.
A vending machine: It has states (Idle, Has Money, Dispensing). Going from one state to another depends on inputs (button pressed, coins inserted). Output (soda dispensed) happens at specific states.
A hotel with many rooms (flip-flops). Each guest (bit) gets a room. The clerk (clock) checks everyone in at the same time. All rooms update together - that's a register.
